name: aligned_zero_size_vacuous
reason: If alignment with size 0 is vacuously true, any guard using `aligned(x, 0)` silently passes, admitting invalid states.

name: aligned_off_by_one
code:
proof fn phi_2_aligned_off_by_one(addr: nat)
    requires
        aligned(addr, 8),
    ensures
        aligned(addr + 1, 8),
{
}
reason: If incrementing an 8-aligned address by 1 preserves alignment, the alignment predicate is trivially true and all ptmem write guards are vacuous.

name: page_align_not_word_align
code:
proof fn phi_3_page_align_not_word_align(addr: nat)
    requires
        aligned(addr, 4096),
    ensures
        !aligned(addr, 8),
{
}
reason: Page-alignment (4096) must imply 8-byte alignment since 4096 is a multiple of 8; if it doesn't, pml4 alignment invariants fail to guarantee write alignment.

name: aligned_cross_divisor
code:
proof fn phi_4_aligned_cross_divisor(addr: nat)
    requires
        aligned(addr, 8),
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 3),
{
}
reason: 8-alignment should not entail 3-alignment (gcd(8,3)=1); if it does the modular semantics of `aligned` are broken and non-power-of-two checks become unsound.

name: aligned_below_size_wrong_value
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_below_size_wrong_value(addr: nat)
    requires
        aligned(addr, 4096),
        addr < 4096,
    ensures
        addr == 1,
{
}
reason: The only nat below 4096 that is 4096-aligned is 0; if the solver derives addr==1, the modular-arithmetic definition of `aligned` is inconsistent.
